methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Airship Industries is a seed-stage company operating in the Drones & Autonomous sector. The comparable universe consists of 7 private companies in this space, including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Thoron. No public peers exist in this narrow sector.

The peer-median method is not applicable. The Drones & Autonomous sector contains only 4 same-sector peers total, with zero companies matching Airship's seed stage, zero in adjacent stages, and zero in similar valuation tiers. This filtering yields insufficient datapoints (need ≥3) for reliable median-based valuation. Relying on an all-sector median would introduce substantial methodological distortion given the heterogeneity of business models, technology maturity, and capital requirements within autonomous systems broadly.

Without applicable triangulation methods, this analysis cannot produce a defensible valuation range from the available data. A credible valuation would require either: (i) expanding the comp set to include earlier-stage or adjacent drone/autonomous subsectors; (ii) obtaining additional financial metrics (revenue, burn rate, capital raised) for the identified peers; or (iii) employing bottom-up methods tied to Airship's specific technical milestones and addressable market. The core constraint is the sparseness of seed-stage comparables in this emerging sector rather than the absence of later-stage reference points.
